Title: CP Admin API Access
Description: Verify Kong CP Admin API is accessible and secured
Test steps:
1. From workstation, hit CP Admin API endpoint
2. Verify authentication is required
3. Verify only internal network can reach CP
Expected results:
Admin API requires authentication
CP not exposed externally

Title: DP Proxy Traffic Flow
Description: Verify DP proxies traffic from workstation to demo server
Test steps:
1. Deploy a service+route on Kong pointing to demo server
2. From workstation send request via DP proxy port
3. Confirm request reaches demo server
Expected results:
Traffic flows: Workstation -> Kong DP -> Demo Server
Response returned

Title: PostgreSQL Persistence
Description: Verify CP connects to PostgreSQL and persists config
Test steps:
1. Check Kong CP logs for DB connection
2. Create a route via Admin API
3. Restart Kong CP pod
4. Verify route still exists after restart
Expected results: Config persists in PostgreSQL across restarts

Title: K8s Container Deployment
Description: Verify Kong runs on Kubernetes
Test steps:
1. Check Kong pods status via kubectl
2. Verify CP and DP pods are running
3. Check service endpoints
Expected results:
Both CP and DP pods healthy
Services accessible within cluster

Title: API Key Authentication
Description: Verify Kong enforces API key on routes
Test steps:
1. Enable key-auth plugin on a test route
2. Create a consumer with API key
3. Send with valid key invalid key no key
Expected results:
Valid key -> 200
Invalid key -> 401
No key -> 401

Title: JWT Validation + OAuth2
Description: Verify Kong validates JWT for Open Banking
Test steps:
1. Enable jwt plugin on a route
2. Generate valid JWT (RS256)
3. Test: valid expired tampered wrong issuer
Expected results:
Valid JWT -> 200
All invalid -> 401/403

Title: JWT Scope Enforcement
Description: Verify Kong enforces scope-based access via JWT claims
Test steps:
1. Issue JWT with scope=read_only
2. Hit GET endpoint -> allowed
3. Hit POST endpoint -> denied
Expected results:
read_only scope cannot write
write scope can do both

Title: mTLS Client Certificate
Description: Verify mTLS for partner/client certificate auth
Test steps:
1. Enable mTLS on a route
2. Generate test client cert
3. Send with valid cert expired cert no cert
Expected results:
Valid cert -> 200
Invalid/no cert -> 401

Title: IP Whitelisting
Description: Verify IP restriction on routes
Test steps:
1. Configure IP restriction (allow specific CIDR)
2. Send from allowed IP
3. Send from blocked IP
Expected results:
Allowed IP -> 200
Blocked IP -> 403

Title: CORS + WAF Protection
Description: Verify CORS headers and basic WAF protection
Test steps:
1. Enable CORS plugin on a route
2. Send OPTIONS from allowed/disallowed origin
3. Test with basic SQLi pattern in URL
Expected results:
CORS headers correct for allowed origins
SQLi pattern blocked or escaped

Title: Deny-by-default
Description: Verify unmatched routes return 403 not 200
Test steps:
1. Delete all routes from Kong
2. Hit any path against DP
Expected results: Returns 403 (not 404, not 200)

Title: Rate Limiting (per client)
Description: Verify per-client rate limiting
Test steps:
1. Enable rate-limiting plugin (5 req/min)
2. Send 7 requests as same consumer
3. Wait 1 min send again
Expected results:
First 5 -> 200
6th+7th -> 429
After cooldown -> 200

Title: Rate Limiting (per route + quota)
Description: Verify per-route rate limit and daily quota
Test steps:
1. Set route limit 100 req/min + daily quota 500
2. Multiple consumers hit same route
3. Exceed daily quota
Expected results:
Route cap: aggregate > 100 -> 429
Daily quota: > 500 -> 429 across all consumers
Quota resets next day

Title: Throttling + Spike Arrest
Description: Verify burst control and traffic smoothing
Test steps:
1. Enable rate-limiting with burst=10 sustained=2/sec
2. Send 20 requests in 1 second
3. Observe actual throughput
Expected results:
First 10 pass immediately (burst)
Remaining 10 are queued and released at 2/sec

Title: Circuit Breaker + Backend Retry
Description: Verify Kong protects backend from cascading failure
Test steps:
1. Configure circuit breaker (5 failures / 30s)
2. Make demo server slow/unresponsive
3. Send multiple requests
4. Verify retries then circuit opens
Expected results:
After threshold -> circuit opens -> 503 fast-fail
Cooldown -> half-open -> retry
Retry configured attempts before giving up

Title: Response Caching
Description: Verify Kong caches responses reduces backend load
Test steps:
1. Enable proxy-cache plugin
2. Send same GET request twice
3. Compare latency and response source
Expected results:
Second request served from cache (X-Cache-Status: Hit)
Latency significantly lower on cached response

Title: Access Logging
Description: Verify Kong logs all proxy requests
Test steps:
1. Enable http-log plugin to file/stdout
2. Send several test requests
3. Verify log fields: timestamp IP method path status latency consumer_id
Expected results:
Complete request log for every request
Fields match expected format

Title: Correlation ID + Audit Trail
Description: Verify end-to-end tracing via correlation ID
Test steps:
1. Enable correlation-id plugin
2. Send request with custom correlation ID header
3. Check same ID appears in Kong logs and backend logs
Expected results:
Same correlation ID across all services
Config changes logged with who+when

Title: Prometheus Metrics + Alerting
Description: Verify Kong exposes metrics for monitoring and alerting
Test steps:
1. Enable prometheus plugin
2. Hit /metrics endpoint
3. Check metric fields
4. Set threshold alert on error rate
Expected results:
Metrics include: requests_total latency_ms connections
Alert triggers when error rate > threshold

Title: SIEM Log Forwarding
Description: Verify Kong forwards logs to external collector
Test steps:
1. Configure http-log plugin to external endpoint
2. Send test requests
3. Verify logs received at external endpoint
Expected results: External log collector receives Kong logs in real-time
Feature Checklist: SIEM Integration (Splunk/ELK)[Note: needs external log endpoint]

Title: Path-based Routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on request path
Test steps:
1. Configure /api/demo1 -> endpoint A
2. Configure /api/demo2 -> endpoint B
3. Send requests to each path
Expected results: Each path routes to correct backend endpoint

Title: Host-based Routing
Description: Verify Kong routes based on Host header
Test steps:
1. Configure api.fubon-test.com -> demo A
2. Configure portal.fubon-test.com -> demo B
3. Send with different Host headers
Expected results: Each hostname routes to correct backend

Title: Load Balancing + HA
Description: Verify Kong load balances across multiple targets
Test steps:
1. Configure upstream with 2 demo server instances
2. Send 10 requests
3. Take one target down
4. Continue sending requests
Expected results:
Requests distributed round-robin
If one target down -> traffic to remaining only
No dropped requests during failover

Title: Request/Response Transformation
Description: Verify Kong transforms headers and body
Test steps:
1. Enable header-transformation plugin
2. Add/remove/modify request headers
3. Transform JSON -> XML (if needed)
Expected results:
Headers modified as configured
Body transformation works if backend expects specific format

Title: Backend Protocol (HTTPS/TCP)
Description: Verify Kong routes to HTTPS and TCP backends
Test steps:
1. Configure route to HTTPS backend with cert
2. Configure route to TCP backend (non-HTTP)
3. Verify both work
Expected results:
HTTPS backend: TLS termination works
TCP backend: raw TCP proxied correctly

Title: API Versioning
Description: Verify multiple API versions coexist
Test steps:
1. Deploy /v1/accounts and /v2/accounts with different logic
2. Call both versions
3. Deprecate v1 verify v2 still works
Expected results:
Both versions serve correctly
v1 can be deprecated without breaking v2

Title: gRPC Support
Description: Verify Kong proxies gRPC calls
Test steps:
1. Configure gRPC route to gRPC backend
2. Send unary gRPC call
3. Send streaming gRPC call
Expected results:
gRPC traffic proxied correctly
Both unary and streaming work
Feature Checklist: gRPC Support[Note: needs gRPC backend on demo server]

Title: SOAP/XML Support
Description: Verify Kong proxies SOAP/XML services
Test steps:
1. Configure route to SOAP endpoint
2. Send SOAP envelope request
3. Verify XML response
Expected results:
SOAP request reaches backend
XML response returned correctly
Feature Checklist: SOAP Support[Note: needs SOAP endpoint on demo server]

Title: Declarative Config via decK
Description: Verify Kong works with declarative YAML config (GitOps)
Test steps:
1. Export Kong config via decK to YAML
2. Modify config (add new route)
3. Run deck validate
4. Import config back
5. Verify new route works
Expected results:
Export -> modify -> validate -> import cycle works
deck validate catches syntax errors before apply
Feature Checklist: Config as Code, CI/CD Integration, Policy Versioning

Title: Config Validation + Rollback
Description: Verify config validation and quick rollback
Test steps:
1. Export current good config via decK
2. Apply deliberately broken config
3. Verify kong rejects it
4. Rollback to previous good config
5. Verify services restored
Expected results:
Broken config rejected with clear error
Rollback restores previous working state

Title: Blue/Green Deployment
Description: Verify zero-downtime upstream switch
Test steps:
1. Configure blue upstream (current)
2. Deploy green upstream (new version)
3. Switch Kong from blue to green
4. Monitor for dropped requests during switch
Expected results:
Upstream switch completes without dropped requests
Green upstream handles traffic after switch
Can revert to blue if needed

Title: Environment Promotion
Description: Verify config promoted across environments
Test steps:
1. Export config from dev/UAT
2. Import to another environment
3. Verify all routes work in target environment
Expected results:
Config promotes cleanly between environments
No environment-specific hardcoding issues

Title: Claim-based Routing + ABAC
Description: Verify Kong routes based on JWT claims for RBAC/ABAC
Test steps:
1. Create consumer with role=admin and role=user
2. Route: /admin requires role=admin claim
3. Test admin JWT -> /admin allowed
4. Test user JWT -> /admin denied
Expected results:
admin JWT -> /admin 200 /user 200
user JWT -> /admin 403 /user 200

Title: Context-based Decision
Description: Verify Kong uses dynamic context for access decisions
Test steps:
1. Configure plugin to check transaction amount header
2. TXN > 10000 -> require extra authorization header
3. Test under and over threshold
Expected results:
Small txn -> passes normally
Large txn (>10K) -> extra auth required

Title: External Policy Engine (OPA)
Description: Verify Kong integrates with OPA for policy decisions
Test steps:
1. Deploy OPA sidecar/service
2. Configure Kong to call OPA for auth decisions
3. Test allow and deny policies
Expected results:
Kong defers to OPA for policy decisions
OPA allow -> request proceeds -> OPA deny -> 403
Feature Checklist: External Policy Engine (OPA/PDP)[Note: needs OPA deployed]

Title: Kafka/MQ Integration
Description: Verify Kong publishes events to message queue
Test steps:
1. Configure Kafka/MQ plugin
2. Send API request
3. Verify event published to queue topic
Expected results: API call triggers message in configured queue topic

Title: Hybrid Deployment
Description: Verify same Kong config works on-prem + cloud
Test steps:
1. Export config from on-prem Kong
2. Import into cloud Kong instance
3. Verify routes work identically
Expected results:
Same config works in both environments
Routes plugins services identical

Title: Multi-Region DR
Description: Verify Kong can route to alternate region
Test steps:
1. Configure primary + DR upstream
2. Health check detects primary down
3. Verify traffic routes to DR region
Expected results: Traffic fails over to DR region automatically

Title: API Portal Access
Description: Verify Kong Dev Portal is accessible
Test steps:
1. Access Kong Dev Portal URL
2. Browse API catalogue
3. Register as new developer
Expected results:
Portal shows API catalogue
Developer can register account
Feature Checklist: API Portal[Note: needs Kong Portal deployed]

Title: API Documentation
Description: Verify Swagger/OpenAPI docs auto-generated
Test steps:
1. Publish API spec to Dev Portal
2. Browse API documentation
3. Check request/response examples
Expected results: API docs show endpoints params and examples
Feature Checklist: API Documentation[Note: needs Kong Portal deployed]

Title: API Testing Console
Description: Verify built-in test console works
Test steps:
1. Open API in Dev Portal
2. Use Try-It-Out console
3. Send test request from portal
Expected results: Test console sends real API request and shows response
Feature Checklist: API Testing Tools[Note: needs Kong Portal deployed]

Title: SDK Generation
Description: Verify auto-generated client SDK
Test steps:
1. Navigate to SDK download in Portal
2. Select language (Python/Java/Go)
3. Download and inspect SDK
Expected results:
SDK generated for selected language
Contains correct API endpoints and models
Feature Checklist: SDK Generation[Note: needs Kong Portal deployed]

Title: Subscription Management
Description: Verify developers can request API keys
Test steps:
1. Register as developer in portal
2. Create new application
3. Request API key for specific API
4. Use generated key to call API
Expected results:
API key generated and associated with developer
Key works for accessing subscribed APIs
Feature Checklist: Subscription Mgmt[Note: needs Kong Portal deployed]

Title: Kong Latency Overhead
Description: Measure Kong added latency
Test steps:
1. Ping demo server directly (no Kong) baseline
2. Send same request via Kong DP
3. Calculate difference
4. Record p50 p95 p99
Expected results: Kong overhead < 5ms (p99)

Title: Throughput Test
Description: Verify Kong handles expected TPS
Test steps:
1. Send parallel requests via Postman/script
2. Ramp up from low to target (e.g. 500 TPS)
3. Monitor Kong CPU and memory on K8s
Expected results:
No errors at expected TPS
CPU < 70% memory stable
